touches.ai_summary: Inbound inquiry from Aria Wright, VP of Sales at RubySoftware. Expressed interest in call recording and coaching solution for their sales team. Currently evaluating options including Gong. Requested information on pricing and features for ~10 reps.

questions_asked.question: What does your call recording and coaching platform offer compared to Gong?
questions_asked.answer: Positioned our unlimited automation tier, stronger coaching workflows, and more flexible pricing model for SMB teams.

next_steps: Send pricing overview and feature comparison

touches.ai_summary: Sent follow-up email with pricing details for unlimited automation tier at $880/seat annually (10 seats = $8,800 ACV). Included feature comparison highlighting coaching workflows and automation capabilities. Aria responded positively, asked to schedule a discovery call.

questions_asked.question: Can we jump on a call this week to discuss our specific use case?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed availability and sent calendar invite for discovery call.

touches.ai_summary: 10-minute discovery call with Aria. RubySoftware has 10 sales reps struggling with coaching consistency. Currently manual call reviews are time-consuming. Evaluating Gong and our solution. Timeline: wants to decide within 4-6 weeks. Budget allocated for sales tools this quarter.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the search for a call recording solution right now?
questions_asked.answer: New sales hires ramping slowly, need better coaching and onboarding process.

questions_asked.question: Who else is involved in this decision?
questions_asked.answer: I'm leading the evaluation. Will need to get final sign-off from our CRO before purchase.

next_steps: Send demo link and product overview

touches.ai_summary: Sent email with personalized demo environment and documentation focused on coaching workflows and automation features. Included case study from similar-sized SaaS company. Aria acknowledged receipt and confirmed she'll review before technical eval.
next_steps: Technical evaluation scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking in on demo environment access and confirming technical evaluation meeting. Shared agenda covering integration requirements, security/compliance, and coaching workflow demonstration. Aria confirmed attendance and requested we also cover reporting capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Can we make sure to cover your reporting and analytics during the eval?
questions_asked.answer: Added reporting deep-dive to the agenda, will show pipeline impact metrics.

touches.ai_summary: 38-minute technical evaluation with Aria. Walked through platform setup, CRM integration (they use HubSpot), and coaching workflows. Demonstrated automated call scoring and manager dashboards. Aria engaged throughout, tested features hands-on. Raised question about integration complexity and mentioned Gong's partnership with HubSpot. Overall positive response to automation capabilities.

questions_asked.question: How long does the typical HubSpot integration take to set up?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 business days with our implementation team, mostly automated.

questions_asked.question: How does this compare to what you've seen from Gong so far?
questions_asked.answer: Gong demo was good but felt heavy for our team size. Likes our coaching focus better.

questions_asked.question: What does implementation support look like?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM for first 90 days, weekly check-ins, training sessions for managers and reps.

objections: Concerned about adding another tool to the stack - want to make sure the team actually adopts it
next_steps: Aria will discuss internally with CRO

touches.ai_summary: Sent formal proposal for 10 seats at $8,800 ACV, unlimited automation tier. Included implementation timeline, training plan, and ROI calculator. Followed up on CRO discussion. Aria responded that she's still working to get time on CRO's calendar, hopes to present next week.

Check-in email on CRO presentation status. Aria replied that she presented the proposal and CRO is interested but wants to meet directly before approving. Requesting pricing flexibility - asked if we can match Gong's quote. Tone slightly more cautious, timeline extending.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the $880 per seat pricing? Gong came in a bit lower.
questions_asked.answer: Offered to discuss custom pricing on executive call, emphasized value of unlimited automation tier vs Gong's usage-based model.

next_steps: Schedule executive alignment call with Aria and CRO

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting scheduled with Aria and CRO, rescheduled once due to CRO conflict. 36-minute call eventually held with only Aria - CRO had to drop last minute. Discussed pricing, asked for 15% discount to match competitive offer. Aria is champion but acknowledged final decision rests with CRO who hasn't directly engaged yet. Committed to trying to reschedule CRO meeting but no firm date. Deal still active but single-threaded risk evident.

questions_asked.question: What would make the CRO comfortable moving forward without another meeting?
questions_asked.answer: Aria thinks a discount closer to Gong's pricing would help her make the case internally.

questions_asked.question: Can you do $750 per seat to match their offer?
questions_asked.answer: Offered 10% discount to $792/seat ($7,920 total) as best and final, emphasized stronger coaching ROI.

questions_asked.question: When do you need to make a final decision by?
questions_asked.answer: End of quarter ideally, but might slip if CRO doesn't prioritize the decision.

objections: Pricing still higher than Gong's competitive offer
CRO hasn't had direct engagement with our team yet
next_steps: Aria to discuss revised pricing with CRO
